description: Define 93 PPL functions as Unified Function specs (Substrait YAML + test DSL), implement as DataFusion UDFs in Rust, verify end-to-end with PPL queries via SQL transpilation.

userStories.title: Create unified-functions directory structure and shared schema
userStories.description: Create api/src/main/resources/unified-functions/ with extensions/ and tests/ subdirectories. Create test_tables.yaml with 2-3 test tables (logs, employees) including column schemas and sample data. Define the type mapping convention (STRING, INTEGER, LONG, DOUBLE, BOOLEAN, TIMESTAMP, DATE).
acceptanceCriteria: api/src/main/resources/unified-functions/test_tables.yaml exists with logs and employees tables
Each table has columns with name and type, plus sample_data rows

userStories.title: Write Unified Function YAML specs for IP functions (8 functions)
userStories.description: Create unified_ip_functions.yaml with URN extension:org.opensearch:unified_ip_functions. Define specs for: cidrmatch, is_ipv4, is_ipv6, ip_to_int, int_to_ip, ip_version, ip_prefix, geoip. Each function must have: name, description, metadata (null_handling, edge_cases, ai_hints with platform_hints for datafusion Rust crates), and impls with typed args and return type. Follow the design doc at ~/.kiro/plans/opensearch-sql/2026-04-13-ppl-function-spec-on-substrait-design.md for the exact YAML convention.
acceptanceCriteria: All 8 IP functions have complete specs with metadata and ai_hints
YAML is valid and follows Substrait extension format

userStories.title: Write Substrait test DSL for IP functions
userStories.description: Create unified_ip_functions.test with test cases for all 8 IP functions. Use Substrait test DSL format with header ### SUBSTRAIT_SCALAR_TEST: v1.0 and ### SUBSTRAIT_INCLUDE: extension:org.opensearch:unified_ip_functions. Each function needs >=10 test cases covering: basic happy path (>=3), null handling (all nullable args), edge cases (invalid input, boundaries, empty strings). Follow the design doc for exact DSL syntax.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of 8 IP functions has >=10 test cases
Test groups include: basic, null_handling, edge_cases
Uses correct Substrait test DSL syntax with typed literals

userStories.title: Write YAML specs for JSON functions (9 functions)
userStories.description: Create unified_json_functions.yaml for: json_extract, json_valid, json_keys, json_delete, json_set, json_append, json_extend, json_array, json_object. Follow same convention as IP functions.
acceptanceCriteria: All 9 JSON functions have complete specs
YAML is valid Substrait extension format

userStories.title: Write Substrait test DSL for JSON functions
userStories.description: Create unified_json_functions.test with >=10 test cases per function. Cover: basic extraction, nested paths, array access, missing keys, invalid JSON, null handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of 9 JSON functions has >=10 test cases
Covers nested data, missing keys, invalid JSON edge cases

userStories.title: Write YAML specs for datetime functions (~30 functions)
userStories.description: Create unified_datetime_functions.yaml for all ~30 PPL-specific datetime functions that have no Calcite standard equivalent: YEARWEEK, WEEKDAY, STRFTIME, CONVERT_TZ, DAYNAME, MONTHNAME, DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP constructors, FROM_DAYS, TO_DAYS, MAKEDATE, MAKETIME, PERIOD_ADD, PERIOD_DIFF, SEC_TO_TIME, TIME_TO_SEC, TIMEDIFF, SYSDATE, UTC_DATE/TIME/TIMESTAMP, ADDTIME, SUBTIME, ADDDATE, SUBDATE, FROM_UNIXTIME, UNIX_TIMESTAMP, DATE_FORMAT, STR_TO_DATE, GET_FORMAT, MICROSECOND, MINUTE_OF_DAY.
acceptanceCriteria: All ~30 datetime functions have complete specs
YAML is valid Substrait extension format

userStories.title: Write Substrait test DSL for datetime functions
userStories.description: Create unified_datetime_functions.test with >=10 test cases per function. Cover: basic usage, null handling, timezone edge cases, boundary dates, format strings.
acceptanceCriteria: Each datetime function has >=10 test cases
Covers timezone, boundary dates, format string edge cases

userStories.title: Write YAML specs and tests for remaining 8 categories
userStories.description: Create YAML specs and test files for: math (8 funcs), condition (2), aggregation (7), collection (11), parse (3), type_conv (8), crypto (1), rex (3), binning (4), relevance (7). That is 8 YAML files + 8 test files. Each function needs spec + >=10 test cases. For relevance functions, note they are pushdown-only.
acceptanceCriteria: All 8 remaining YAML extension files exist in extensions/
All 8 remaining test files exist in tests/
Relevance functions marked as pushdown-only in metadata
userStories.notes: This is a large story but the pattern is established by US-002 through US-007. Batch by category.

userStories.title: Scaffold unified-functions-datafusion Rust crate
userStories.description: Create unified-functions-datafusion/ at repo root with Cargo.toml (deps: datafusion, arrow, serde_yaml, serde_json), src/lib.rs with register_all_udfs(ctx: &SessionContext) entry point, and module stubs for each category (ip.rs, json.rs, datetime.rs, collection.rs, math.rs, parse.rs, condition.rs, aggregation.rs, type_conv.rs, crypto.rs, rex.rs, binning.rs). Also create tests/spec_tests.rs stub. The crate must compile with cargo build.
acceptanceCriteria: Module files exist for all 12 categories

userStories.title: Implement Substrait test DSL parser in Rust
userStories.description: In tests/spec_tests.rs (or a src/test_harness.rs module), implement a parser that reads .test files and executes them against DataFusion. The parser must handle: header lines (### SUBSTRAIT_SCALAR_TEST, ### SUBSTRAIT_INCLUDE), test group comments (# group: description), scalar test lines (func(arg1::type, arg2::type) = expected::type), null literals (null::type), and boolean/string/integer/float/timestamp typed literals. For each test line, generate a SQL SELECT statement, execute via SessionContext::sql(), and compare results.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser reads .test files and extracts test cases
Handles typed literals: str, bool, i32, i64, fp64, timestamp, date
Generates SQL SELECT for each test case
Executes against DataFusion and compares results
cargo test succeeds (with placeholder pass for now)

userStories.title: Implement IP function UDFs in Rust and pass spec tests
userStories.description: Implement all 8 IP functions as DataFusion ScalarUDFs in src/ip.rs. Register them in register_all_udfs(). Use the columnar API pattern: values_to_arrays -> iter -> collect. Use ipnetwork or similar crate for CIDR matching. Run the spec test harness against unified_ip_functions.test — all tests must pass.
acceptanceCriteria: All 8 IP functions implemented as DataFusion ScalarUDFs

userStories.title: Implement JSON function UDFs in Rust and pass spec tests
userStories.description: Implement all 9 JSON functions as DataFusion ScalarUDFs in src/json.rs. Use serde_json for parsing and jsonpath_lib or similar for path extraction. All unified_json_functions.test cases must pass.

userStories.title: Implement datetime function UDFs in Rust and pass spec tests
userStories.description: Implement all ~30 datetime functions as DataFusion ScalarUDFs in src/datetime.rs. Use chrono crate for date/time operations. All unified_datetime_functions.test cases must pass.
userStories.notes: Largest category. May need to split across iterations if too big.

userStories.title: Implement remaining function categories in Rust and pass all spec tests
userStories.description: Implement UDFs for: math (8), condition (2), collection (11), parse (3), type_conv (8), crypto (1), rex (3), binning (4), aggregation (7). Register all in register_all_udfs(). All spec test files must pass. Skip relevance functions (pushdown-only, not evaluable).
acceptanceCriteria: All spec test files pass (except relevance — pushdown-only)
userStories.notes: Large story. Batch by category. Relevance functions are pushdown-only and should NOT be implemented as UDFs.

userStories.title: Implement shared schema loader and PPL E2E tests
userStories.description: In the Rust crate, implement a loader that reads test_tables.yaml and creates DataFusion MemTables with sample data. Create tests/ppl_e2e_tests.rs that takes pre-transpiled SQL (representing PPL queries) and executes them against DataFusion with all UDFs registered. Include 10-20 SQL queries covering all function categories. Queries should be realistic PPL-transpiled SQL like: SELECT *, json_extract(payload, '$.status') AS j FROM logs WHERE cidrmatch(ip, '10.0.0.0/8').
acceptanceCriteria: Schema loader reads test_tables.yaml and creates MemTables
Queries cover IP, JSON, datetime, math, collection, parse categories
